verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is an EGF-family ligand synthesized as a single-pass transmembrane proligand (proEPR) that transits the plasma membrane before ADAM17-mediated ectodomain shedding releases the soluble mature ligand that signals via EGFR/ERBB4. The TM precursor stage is real and supports juxtacrine signaling, but it is short-lived and the soluble shed form is the canonical biological actor and the form engaged by clinical anti-EREG antibody programs. Fits the transient-TM-precursor / shed-ligand-dominant pattern.
